mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in industrial production (INDPRO) signal expanding economic output and rising corporate earnings, which drive positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon regardless of other conditions.
failure_condition: If rolling 12-month correlation between INDPRO growth and subsequent 60-day SPX returns is negative or less than 0.10, hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate below a critical threshold (6.5%) confirms tightening labor market conditions, which supports consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over one month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls below 6.5% within the last 3 months
failure_condition: If UNRATE below 6.5% is followed by negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances over the test period.

mechanism_narrative: The joint condition of very low federal funds rate (below 0.5%) and subdued inflation (CPIAUCSL below 235) indicates highly accommodative monetary policy that boosts equity valuations through low discount rates and ample liquidity, producing positive forward returns.
failure_condition: If on occasions when both conditions hold, the subsequent 20-day SPX return is lower than the unconditional mean, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: The effect of GDP growth on equity returns changes sign depending on the business cycle phase. During expansions (as in 2013), above-trend GDP growth (annualized >3%) signals overheating and monetary tightening expectations, depressing equities. During recessions, positive growth signals recovery and drives positive returns. Given the current expansion, strong GDP growth is negative.
failure_condition: If any expansion period with GDP growth >3% yields positive 60-day returns in over half of cases, or if the direction flip between regimes is not statistically significant.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail and food services sales (RSAFS) indicate robust consumer demand and spending, which directly boosts corporate revenues and earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over a one-month horizon regardless of other conditions.
failure_condition: If the rank IC of RSAFS month-over-month growth with 20-day forward returns is below 0.05 over any rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A surge in housing starts above 1,000 thousand units signals a robust construction sector, which has strong multiplier effects on employment and related industries, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: If HOUST > 1,000 leads to negative 60-day returns in more than 40% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strong monthly payroll growth (PAYEMS > 150k) and a declining unemployment rate (UNRATE down >0.2% over 3 months) provide a comprehensive confirmation of labor market health, which supports consumption and corporate profits, yielding positive 20-day equity returns.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly change > 150k and UNRATE 3-month change < -0.2pp
failure_condition: If the joint condition yields a 20-day return distribution that is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: At the zero lower bound, the absolute level of the federal funds rate has little economic impact, but its stability or any slight increase signals a shift away from emergency accommodation, which can be interpreted by markets as a vote of confidence in the economy, leading to positive short-term returns. Given current near-zero rates, a stable or slightly higher FEDFUNDS is positive.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS held at 0.08% or below for at least 1 month
failure_condition: If SPX 5-day returns are negative in more than 50% of instances when FEDFUNDS remains at 0.08% or lower.
